registration.registered_by: docs/OPH_FALSIFICATION_PROGRAM.md, forward W/Z note: the pulls are one-loop values; the preregistered 2026-07-14 sweep exhausted the one-loop menu, and the two-loop repair is the registered next computation
registration.predecessor_artifacts_frozen: the 2026-07-14 menu and results files are read-only inputs; this computation edits neither them nor any one-loop lane module

one_loop_truncation_sites.transport: run_alphas_from_unification applies the one-loop MSSM triple from mu_U down; it enters (a) the mZ fixed point, (b) the pixel-residual alpha_U selector, (c) the readout couplings alpha2(mz_run) and alphaY(mz_run)
one_loop_truncation_sites.transmutation: v/E_star = exp(-2 pi / (beta_EW alpha_U)) / sqrt(P) carries no declared two-loop form
one_loop_truncation_sites.matching: the readout is the tree two-law evaluation; z_stage3 delta_rho = 3/(32 pi^2) is the single declared scheme switch

engine.certification: validate() executed 2026-07-16 before this spec was written: endpoint_ok all true against the Buttazzo et al. 1307.3536 three-loop M_Pl endpoint, lambda zero crossing in (1e9, 1e12) GeV, one-loop reference set matched to 1e-15; all_ok true
engine.normalization_note: the engine state variable g1 is GUT-normalized, g1 = sqrt(5/3) * g_Y; Buttazzo et al. eq. 60 quotes g_Y(Mt) = 0.35830 and needs the sqrt(5/3) factor before entering the state vector; the lane alpha1 is already GUT-normalized so alpha1_engine = alpha1_lane and alphaY = 0.6 * alpha1
engine.content_limit: the engine carries Standard Model field content only; no MSSM two-loop coefficients exist in any certified module of this repository

revision_declaration.summary: the one-loop transport of the gauge couplings between mz_run and mu_U is corrected by the certified two-loop increment of the SM engine; every other chain object is unchanged
couplings_running_at_two_loops: yt (top Yukawa, feed per declared branch)
lam (Higgs quartic, feed per declared branch)
revision_declaration.matching_relations_changed: none; the tree two-law readout, the transmutation law, the pixel-residual selector formula, the eta_source reduction eta = alpha_U * (1 - 2 sin2thetaW0) with sin2 = alphaY/(alphaY+alpha2), and the clock display adapter are all retained exactly

increment_construction.reason_for_upward_runs: an SM-content trajectory holding unification at alpha_U has no real alpha3 below about 8.5e6 GeV (structural failure recorded in the frozen 2026-07-14 sweep), so the increment is computed on upward runs from the mz_run boundary
increment_construction.boundary: at mu = mz_run (the one-loop fixed point of the candidate chain): g_i = sqrt(4 pi alpha_i_lane(mz_run)) with alpha1 GUT-normalized, plus (yt, lam) per branch
increment_construction.runs: two integrations of the engine from ln(mz_run) to ln(mu_U) with identical boundary, identical RK4 step count, loops=1 and loops=2
increment_construction.increment: Delta_i = invalpha_i_2loop(mu_U) - invalpha_i_1loop(mu_U) for i in {1,2,3}
increment_construction.application: unification at mu_U at the chain alpha_U is preserved, so the low end absorbs the increment with opposite sign: invalpha_i_repaired(mz_run) = invalpha_i_lane(mz_run) - Delta_i
increment_construction.one_loop_reference_note: at one loop the gauge beta functions carry no Yukawa or quartic term, so the loops=1 reference run is insensitive to the (yt, lam) boundary; the difference isolates the two-loop terms

revision_declaration.evaluation_scale: repaired couplings are evaluated at the one-loop fixed point mz_run of the same candidate chain; a two-loop re-solve of the fixed point itself is second order in the correction and is gated open
revision_declaration.zero_tunable_elements: this revision contains zero tunable elements; every scale, boundary, step count, window, and branch below is fixed in this document before any two-loop lane number exists, and every declared branch is emitted

yukawa_feed.rule: the lane D11 boundary: yt(mu_U) sits on the critical surface critical_surface_yukawa(gY(mu_U), g2(mu_U)) evaluated on the same two-loop trajectory endpoint, and lam(mu_U) = 0; (yt, lam)(mz_run) are obtained by damped Newton shooting on the loops=2 run, and the converged boundary is reused verbatim in the loops=1 reference run
yt(mz_run) = 0 and lam(mz_run) = 0 in both runs; pure gauge two-loop increment

tier.rule: alpha_U is re-solved so that the pixel residual vanishes on the repaired couplings: for candidate alpha_U the one-loop chain gives mz_run and the boundary, the increment gives repaired alpha2 and alpha3 at mz_run, and pixel_residual(alpha2_rep, alpha3_rep, P) = 0 selects alpha_U; v/E_star is then recomputed from the selected alpha_U; this is the full two-loop forward emission

alpha_U, mu_U, mz_run, and v/E_star are held at the frozen baseline; only the readout couplings alpha2 and alphaY at mz_run receive the increment; this isolates the certified two-loop shift on the couplings entering the W/Z readout

discrete_branch_menu.primary_display_cell: tier_B_full_resolve | ytcs | zero_selector | z_tree (same law and scheme as the baseline chain key)
discrete_branch_menu.cells_emitted: all 16 combinations; none is selected after results exist

numerical_settings_fixed_before_computat.integrator: fixed-step RK4, run() of the engine, n_steps = 8000 per integration (the engine certification step count)

newton_shooting.jacobian: forward finite differences, relative step 1e-6 with absolute floor 1e-8
newton_shooting.failure_rule: fail closed; the ytcs branch is gated and reported unconverged, never substituted

alpha_U_root_finding.bracket_scan: 41 equally spaced points, mirroring solve_alpha_u_from_p of the lane
alpha_U_root_finding.memoization: residual values are cached per alpha_U bit pattern; cached evaluation is exact re-reading, never approximation
alpha_U_root_finding.failure_rule: fail closed; if no sign change exists in the window the tier_B branch is gated with the scan recorded, mirroring the frozen sweep failure protocol

numerical_settings_fixed_before_computat.precision: float64 throughout the transport; the two-law evaluator uses its own exact Fraction internals unchanged; no module-level mpmath state is touched

outputs.landing_criterion: MW inside 80.3692 +- 0.0133 GeV AND MZ inside 91.1880 +- 0.0020 GeV simultaneously, identical to the frozen menu
outputs.direction_verdict_rule: evaluated on the primary display cell only: toward_measurement if both |MW_offset| and |MZ_offset| shrink relative to the baseline cell, away_from_measurement if both grow, mixed otherwise; toward supports the missing-correction reading of CL-5, away kills this repair branch

fail_closed_gates_declared_open: GATE-2L-01 mssm_two_loop_coefficients_unavailable: the increment carries SM field content over the whole [mz_run, mu_U] range; no certified MSSM two-loop engine exists in the repository
GATE-2L-02 transmutation_exponent_one_loop_form: beta_EW alpha_U in the transmutation law has no declared two-loop completion; retained as declared
GATE-2L-03 tree_readout_matching: no two-loop pole-mass or delta_rho matching beyond the declared z_stage3 switch is computed
GATE-2L-04 fixed_point_scale_one_loop: the mZ fixed-point scale is the one-loop solution; the two-loop re-solve of the evaluation scale is second order and uncomputed
GATE-2L-05 no_threshold_insertions: the lane declares no intermediate thresholds and none are introduced
rules_of_record: no scale, threshold, or branch choice is made after any result exists; the menu above is closed and every cell is emitted
the one-loop lane modules and the frozen 2026-07-14 artifacts are never edited
a landing or a direction verdict on the primary cell counts the discrete selections declared here and closes nothing by itself

spec_sha256.scope: computed over the canonical serialization of this document with the spec_sha256 field removed
